# Transcrição: 06-Your EIN

## [00:00 - 03:03]

Hi everybody, Greg Christiansen here from Masterline Law Group. I've gotten quite a few questions over the last week or so about employer identification numbers. They're also called EIN. So when you set up a LLC or a corporation, one of the first things you want to do is go to the Internal Revenue Service, the IRS. You want to obtain what's called an EIN. An EIN is basically a Social Security number for the business. It's a tax identification number for the business. So what we're trying to accomplish here is to keep our personal lives separate and distinct from an entity known as an LLC or a corporation. And in order to do that, we want to have as much separation as possible. And one of the ways we do that is we go to the IRS and we get this EIN. And now when we file for things or we try to open things, we use that instead of our own Social Security number. So for example, when I go and I open a bank account for my LLC or corporation, I will oftentimes take what's called an SS4 form. That's actually what an EIN comes on. If the IRS, if you can go up on their website, if you go and try to buy for an EIN, you'll see that it's form SS4. So when I go into a bank to open up a bank account for my new business, I need to have that SS4 form because it has your EIN number. And you'll see it when you're looking at the form, it'll be in the upper right-hand corner. It'll be a couple of digits with slash and following by seven more digits. So nine total digits, kind of like a Social Security number, but in different formats so they can identify it. Where else will you use an EIN? You'll use an EIN. Some of my clients like to get credit cards for their business. And when they apply for the credit card application, it will ask for their EIN. And so they'll give that to them. When you enter a contractual agreement with somebody else, they'll not request you to fill out a W2 form, which is for employment. They'll ask you to fill out what's called the W9 form. And in there, they'll ask for your, if you have a business, what your EIN is. Because again, you don't want to operate, you've set up the business, you don't want to operate that relationship as an employee. You don't want to operate as a sole practitioner. So you don't want to use your Social Security number for that. They'll use EIN for that. So when you fill out documents on behalf of the entity, business, LLC, or corporation, you'll use this EIN. So at the end of the year, when you file taxes, you'll disclose the EIN in your business to the IRS. And that's how I'll identify that the income that you've driven from your business transactions is through the business and not you personally. So again, what we're trying to do is separate. So we have over here on one side is you as a personal person. And on the other side is a business. We want to keep those separate. And so as we do that, just understand that the reason we're trying to do that is because

## [03:03 - 05:27]

we're trying to make sure that somebody doesn't come to us and say, the business and the person are one person. And so we should set aside the business and make them liable for everything else. Both, and who are we talking about? We're talking about people who may sue the business, creditors that may have entered into a contractual relationship and they sue you for breach of contract or the Internal Revenue Service who's looking for taxes. So that's a public credit, IRS, private credit, company suing you for breach of contract. So an EIN is extremely important as part of your business model. Now in applying for the EIN, we need to have at least one of the owners to use their social security number to get the EIN. So what we do, we get online and you don't need to know the semantics of that. That's why you hire an attorney or a law firm that's confident to do this. But go online, you fill out the information and that information is going to talk about a responsible party. And in that responsible party, we use their social security number to get the EIN. So if something happens to the corporation and nobody responds, they have somebody to go to to say, what's the status on this? We want to send you a notification. When you get that SS4 form, you will notice that somebody's name will be right underneath the business name and the address will be whatever address you use for business or personal or otherwise. But it's okay. These documents are not readily available to the public. It's not like it's something the IRS is giving out to people. So if you do have a name that's associated with the EIN and it is tied to your social, they're not going to tax that if you treat it right. They know they're going to tax the EIN. They just need to know who they can contact if they have questions and that's what they ask for. Hey, part of what we do here at Mastermind Law Group is we just do training and education. We hold ourselves out not just as a law firm that provides the services for you, but we're also a law firm that helps you understand how to utilize the documents and instruments that you've received from us. So part of this content is educational. I'm trying to help you understand the purpose and meaning of having an EIN. How you use it and what the necessity of that is.
